revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兵主大社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hyozu Taisha | image = File:Shiga Hyouzu.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35.115278|N|136.010111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Gojo 566, Yasu City, Shiga Prefecture, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} (as Yachihoko no Kami) * {{ill|Chiyou|lt=Hyozu-no-Kami|ja|兵主神|en|Chiyou|ru|Чи Ю|fr|Chiyou|es|Chi You|zh|蚩尤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | established = {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō (era)|1=Yōrō}} 2 (718 CE) | festivals = May 5 | former status = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | prefecture= {{ill|Yasu|qid=Q611642|1=Yasu, Shiga}} }}
'''Hyozu Taisha''' ({{nihongo|兵主大社|ひょうずたいしゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|qid=Q611642|lt=Yasu|1=Yasu, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and held the former Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} under the name Yachihoko no Kami as its primary deity, along with {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|qid=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}} as auxiliary deities.
== History == According to the shrine's legend, the deity was first enshrined during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} at {{ill|Anashi|ja|穴師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture). It was later moved to its current location in {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō (era)|1=Yōrō}} 2 (718 CE).
Hyozu Taisha gained prominence in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, receiving the Rank of {{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the medieval period, it became a significant shrine for warriors, with patrons including {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}.
The shrine’s grounds include a nationally designated {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Place of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|qid=Q11414752}} garden, which is a typical example of a {{ill|Strolling garden|lt=Strolling garden|lt=Strolling garrden|ja|回遊式庭園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/strolling garrden|qq=}}.
== Grounds == * main hall ({{ill|Honden|ja|main hall|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}): A city-designated cultural property rebuilt in the Edo period. * Worship Hall ({{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|qid=Q3125745|lt=Haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}) * Main Gate ({{ill|Rōmon|ja|楼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōmon|1=Rōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97378762}}): Rebuilt in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it is a prefecturally designated cultural property. * Garden: A nationally designated {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Place of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|qid=Q11414752}}, it is a pond-centered stroll garden. * Old Goma Hall: Previously part of the temple associated with the shrine during the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period. * Auxiliary and subsidiary shrines: These include shrines to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|qid=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}} and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Silk-wrapped haramaki armor (National Museum of Kyoto) * Bronze floral plaques and embroidered banners (Nara National Museum)
=== National Scenic Beauty === * Hyozu Shrine Garden
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Rōmon Gate * Iron bell (Momoyama period)
== Access == * From {{ill|Yasu Station (Shiga)|en|Yasu Station (Shiga)|qid=Q1042474|lt=Yasu Station|1=Yasu Station (Shiga)}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|qid=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}, take either: * The {{ill|Yasu City Community Bus|ja|野洲市コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu City Community Bus|1=Yasu City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11645793}} (south exit) to Gojo Jichikaikan stop, then a 3-minute walk. * {{ill|Ōmi Railway Bus|ja|近江鉄道バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Railway Bus|1=Ohmi Railway Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129172682}} (north exit) to Hyozu Taisha Mae stop, then a 5-minute walk.
* On-site parking: 40 standard vehicles, 5 large buses.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|qid=Q611642|lt=Yasu, Shiga|1=Yasu, Shiga}} * {{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyōzu Shrine|ja|兵主大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}} * [[Itatehyōzu Shrine]]
== External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=488 Shiga Shrine Association entry]
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|兵主大社|version=104453886|comment=Imported full ja history}}
